description:
src/lib.rs:222 — `put` POSTs to https://api.doppler.com/v3/configs/config/secret (singular) with body {project, config, name, value}.

The Doppler API for updating secrets is POST https://api.doppler.com/v3/configs/config/secrets (plural) with body {project, config, secrets: {NAME: VALUE}} (or change_requests). Confirmed against:
  1. Official Doppler API reference: https://docs.doppler.com/reference/secrets-update
  2. Official Doppler CLI source: github.com/DopplerHQ/cli pkg/http/api.go SetSecrets() — uses /v3/configs/config/secrets

Trigger: any caller invoking DopplerBackend::put() against real api.doppler.com.
Bad behavior: the request will return 404 Not Found (no such endpoint) or 400 Bad Request (no such field 'name'). The current code maps 4xx to SecretError::Backend, so the caller gets a generic 'HTTP 404' message with no clue that the endpoint itself is wrong. The crate registers WritableBackendRegistration unconditionally, so any consumer dispatching by URI will hit this.

The crate's own module doc (src/lib.rs:5-8) says 'Not yet integration-tested' — this bug shipped because tests never ran against real Doppler.

Fix: change endpoint to /v3/configs/config/secrets, body to serde_json::json!({"project": ..., "config": ..., "secrets": {self.name: v}}).
title: put POSTs to wrong Doppler endpoint with wrong body shape (broken against real API)
